reviews.review_msg: Wrongfully accused of a bunch of students for using AI for assignments. Used scare tactics to urge students to confess. Avoid at all costs.

You have to get 50/100 in the exam to pass the course as a whole regardless of how well you did in the quizzes and assignments. The final was really really tough! 31 pages, with different declarations and shenanigans. I really regret taking the course as it messed me up from graduating this summer.
Course is really math heavy and the prof just reads of the slides with no examples. He says if you dont understand look at it abit more, you might understand it then. Really hard final (31 pages).

The course is really difficult material-wise and a big jump compared to most courses at SFU. There is some disconect between the level Dr. Li expects students to be at vs. what the average student, so it can be hard to follow lectures. Final was super hard (avg. was < 50), but the curve was very generous and class avg was a B. I learned a ton

Prof does practically no examples of formulas in class and just over teaches theory. Has a boring voice and only gets some attendance because of his class quiz participation. Extremely difficult assignments and final had to get scaled.

The course is ideal for students who aim to squander time, lack motivation, and end up disappointed.

The instructor's approach to teaching was extremely insufficient. The presentation slides in the lectures lacked proper annotations and details (no explanation!), which made it difficult for students to follow along with the concepts being taught.

A strike disrupted this semester, leading to a two-week lecture gap and a challenging final exam. The professor wrapped up the intense material in the last two weeks(make up for the gap) with limited practice or examples. To succeed, students had to comprehend theories derived from these equations for the extensive 35-page finals.

1. He just read from the slides without providing enough context or detail. You'll constantly hear words like "You know" and similar ones.
2. The slides presented are poor in quality. Equations and concepts are not explained clearly.
3. Errors happened in different areas during the course, including all quizzes, assignments, grades, and solutions.

No textbook for non-review part of the course; prof was regularly late for classes and made us stay overtime. The material is very difficult (all math, virtually no coding). Going to lectures does not help at all - they mostly consist of "take this and that.. you know.. right? hm, well, ok moving on". So be prepared to study everything on your own.

It was a tough subject, made even tougher by Ke Li. He thinks everyone in the class is a math genius and teaches high-level math in the course.Tough assignments and quizzes. Will make you do unnecessary stuff like writing assignments in Latex.
